report_name: Everest Ransomware Awakens Sleeping PCs with Wake-on-LAN to Maximize Attack Surface

threat_actors.name: Everest
threat_actors.description: A double-extortion ransomware group active since late 2020. The group has been observed using Wake-on-LAN (WoL) functionality to power on dormant computers on a victim's network to maximize the number of encrypted devices.

attack_patterns.name: Remote System Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The Everest ransomware parses the local Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) cache to discover IP and MAC addresses of other devices on the local network, including those that are offline or sleeping.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: The Everest ransomware disables Windows Defender's Controlled Folder Access feature to evade defenses and allow unauthorized file modification.

attack_patterns.name: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
attack_patterns.description: The Everest ransomware enables File and Printer Sharing and re-enables the legacy SMBv1 protocol to facilitate its propagation and encryption of network shares.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: After waking dormant devices using Wake-on-LAN and preparing the network, the Everest ransomware maps all discovered local and remote network shares and proceeds with file encryption.

attack_patterns.name: File Deletion
attack_patterns.description: After encryption, the Everest ransomware executes a self-deletion script to erase its own executable, hindering forensic analysis.

malware.name: Everest
malware.description: A ransomware variant used by the Everest group that incorporates Wake-on-LAN (WoL) functionality to power on and encrypt dormant network endpoints. It also disables security features and enables insecure protocols to facilitate lateral movement and impact.

indicators.name: Suspicious Wake-on-LAN Broadcast Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Wake-on-LAN Broadcast Activity
id: 7e8b1c2d-9a3b-4f5e-8d7c-6a5b4c3d2e1f
status: experimental
description: Detects a potential spike in Wake-on-LAN (WoL) 'magic packets' sent over UDP ports 7 or 9. This activity was observed being used by Everest ransomware to awaken dormant machines before encryption.
references:
  - https://cyberpress.org/everest-wakes-dormant-hosts/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1018
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationPort:
      - 7
      - 9
    Protocol: 'udp'
    Initiated: 'true'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic consistent with Wake-on-LAN (WoL) magic packets, which Everest ransomware uses to power on sleeping devices.

indicators.name: ARP Cache Discovery Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: ARP Cache Discovery Command Execution
id: f8a1b2c3-d4e5-4f6a-7b8c-9d0e1f2a3b4c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of 'arp -a' to display the ARP cache. This is used by Everest ransomware as a discovery technique to find potential targets on the network.
references:
  - https://cyberpress.org/everest-wakes-dormant-hosts/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1018
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\arp.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' -a'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the `arp -a` command, which Everest ransomware uses to discover potential targets on the local network.

indicators.name: Everest Ransomware SMBv1 Enablement
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Everest Ransomware SMBv1 Enablement Registry Modification
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects registry modification to enable the insecure SMBv1 protocol, a tactic used by Everest ransomware to facilitate lateral movement.
references:
  - https://cyberpress.org/everest-wakes-dormant-hosts/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1021.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|endswith: '\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters\SMB1'
    Details: '1'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the re-enabling of the legacy SMBv1 protocol via registry modification, a technique used by Everest ransomware to prepare for lateral movement.
